key_insight: Accurate inclusion of exact exchange is essential to capture electronic localization, which stabilizes monoclinic BiVO4 and explains its symmetry-breaking distortion and optoelectronic behavior.
engineering_relevance: Selecting the right exchange-correlation functional can materially improve phase stability prediction and band-gap estimates for oxide photocatalysts and related functional materials.
method: Compared DFT treatments with and without exact exchange and spin-orbit coupling.
Computed dense Brillouin-zone band structures for monoclinic and tetragonal BiVO4.
Linked structural distortion to charge transfer between inequivalent oxygen sites and electronic localization.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports systematic comparison across theory levels, dense band-structure sampling, and agreement with the crystal structure and band gap once excitonic and thermal effects are included.
watch_item: Results depend strongly on the chosen exchange-correlation functional, so semi-local DFT may miss the monoclinic ground state and distort predicted electronic properties.
paper_title: Interplay between Electronic Structure, Chemical Bonding, and Lattice Symmetry in Bismuth Vanadate
